title: Mushroom Fruiting
description: A fungal colony seen in cross-section. Mycelium spreads through the substrate as a branching web of fine hyphae; where the web thickens, pins emerge and fruit — the stem elongates and the cap swells from a button to a domed then flattened cap, gills darken underneath, and mature caps drop drifting spores. Caps then age (flattening, curling, darkening; ink caps deliquesce into black drips) and wither back into the litter, while new flushes keep coming. Toadstools, porcini, ink caps and oyster shelves, or a mix. Click to inoculate a fresh flush; beats trigger a colony-wide flush. Growth speed, fruiting density, size, species, spore release, mycelium spread and substrate damp are live and mappable.
